summarization.a: The graph shows the relationship between SF<sub>6</sub> plasma exposure time and EPC (Emission Peak Concentration) in Ångströms.
summarization.b: The graph illustrates the effect of plasma pump time on EPC (Emission Peak Concentration) in Ångströms.
summarization.c: The graph depicts the impact of TMA dosing time on EPC (Emission Peak Concentration) in Ångströms.
summarization.d: The graph shows the influence of TMA pump time on EPC (Emission Peak Concentration) in Ångströms.

d.question: How does EPC depend on the exposure and dosing times shown in the figure?
d.answer: EPC increases with both SF₆ plasma exposure time and TMA dosing time until a saturation level is reached, indicating self-limiting surface reactions. In contrast, EPC shows little dependence on plasma pump time or TMA pump time.

c.question: Does EPC depend strongly on pump times?

a.question: Which two steps show saturation behavior in EPC?
a.answer: SF₆ plasma exposure and TMA dosing.

b.question: Which process parameters control the etching per cycle?
b.answer: SF₆ plasma exposure time, TMA dosing time.
